person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Biography
Richard, the first feudal baron of Bulwick, may have been the son of Richard fitzUrse and the grandson of Urso de Berseres. Other sources have Richard's father as Urse, who held lands in the hundred of Corby in Northamptonshire. Richard was married to Maud (Aubigny) FitzUrse, daughter of Baldwin (Aubigny) d'Aubigny and Sybil (Falaise) d'Aubigny. Together they had three sons, Robert, Walter, Rainald FitzUrse, and two daughters, Margery (FitzUrse) de Engayne and Mabel (Fitzurse) Garnet Richard died sometime after 1158.

(1) Ursa (c1050) Urso, Ours. The progenitor of the family. He had, under the Conqueror, held Grittleton in Wiltshire, of the Abbey of Glastonbury.

(2) Richard FitzUrse (c1090) Richard married Matilda [Maude] de Boulers de Aubigny. She was born in about 1097, the daughter of Baldwin de Boulers [Bollers, Boullers, Bouliers], the Lord of Montgomery, and Sibyl de Falaise.

Richard was a follower of King Stephen in his wars with Empress Matilda. At the battle of Lincoln, in 1141, fighting against the Earls of Gloucester and Chester, he was captured, as was the King.

Barony of Bulwick, Northamptonshire
Sanders writes:
This manor, which became the center of the Fitz Urse honor, does not appear in Domesday Book, while in 1115-18 the Fitz Urse family is not mentioned in association with the estate. However, in 1130 Richard Fitz Urse made payments that are recorded on the Northamptonshire and Essex membranes of the pipe roll. Richard, who was living Christmas 1141-Easter 1142, was followed, ante 1158, by his son Reginald d.1172-5 when his heir was his daughter MAUD.

Children:
1.) Margery FitzUrse (1119), who married Richard Engayne

2.) Reginald FitzUrse (c1125), Had part in the murder of Thomas Becket

3.) Robert FitzUrse (c1130), who inherited his brother's estates and founded the Barham line

4.) Richard FitzUrse III (c1150) ??

5.) Maud FitzUrse (1132), who married a Cantelo

6.) Mabel FitzUrse (1140), who married Benedict Gernet

The name "Urse" means son of the bear. The earliest members of this family are unknown, but it is assumed that a Hastings veteran nicknamed Ursa, "bear," founded the clan. The Family Arms: A bear sable, sometimes depicted as three bears on an argent field.

Research Notes
Historical connections between Urse and this surname.

The first feudal baron of Bulwick. Bulwick Northamptonshire Richard FitzUrse 1130.

Sir Richard's father may have been Richard Fitz Urse, who was married to the widow of Richard Ingaine.
Or the grandson of Urso de Berseres.

Richard's father may be the Urse d'Abetot.

"Richard died sometime after 1158, but before 1166. His heir was his son Reginald. Reginald was one of the four murderers of Thomas Becket".

MORE INFORMATION
Richard fitzUrse was an Anglo-Norman nobleman and feudal baron of Bulwick in Northamptonshire.

Richard may have been the son of Richard fitzUrse, who was married to the widow of Richard Ingaine. Richard may have been the grandson of Urso de Berseres. Other sources give Richard's father as Urse, who held lands in the hundred of Corby in Northamptonshire.

Richard in 1130 was in control of the honour of Bulwick in Northamptonshire. By holding the honour, he is considered to be the first feudal baron of Bulwick.

Richard supported King Stephen of England during the civil war between Stephen and his cousin Empress Matilda over the English throne. Richard was a witness to a charter of Stephen's issued right before the Battle of Lincoln. Richard was captured along with King Stephen during the battle.

Richard married Matilda, daughter of Baldwin de Boulers and Sibyl de Falaise. From the marriage, Richard acquired Worspring and Williton in Somerset. Matilda and Richard had one son, Reginald, and two daughters - Margery and Mabel. Margery married twice - first to Richard Engaine and second to Geoffrey Brito. Mabel was the mother of Roger Gernet.

Richard died sometime after 1158, but before 1166. His heir was his son Reginald. Reginald was one of the four murderers of Thomas Becket.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Richard FitzUrse was born in 1095, in Kirby Bellars, Leicestershire, England as the son of Urse and unknown. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Matilda Boulers. He died from 1140 to 1141, in his hometown.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard FitzUrse(은)는 1095년 영국 레이스스터실르 커비 벨라러스에서 Urse(와)과 unknown의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Matilda Boulers(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1140년 부터 1141년 까지 그의 고향에서 사망했다.
